title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar c values, integer conversions, and size arithmetic decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for checked size and conversion policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.
Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.
Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Integer Ranges Ranks
steps.instruction:
Order the Integer Ranges Ranks evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 6798. Produce bounded C evidence to reject a size calculation that exceeds its bound; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
